title: Students' protests rock Bihar Assembly on final day of Monsoon session
content: Student bodies in Bihar have been triggered by the Cockroach Janta Party's stir in Delhi over paper leak of the NEET medical entrance test

title: ED attaches fresh ₹1,906 crore worth assets of Gameskraft
content: The ED said Gameskraft and RummyTime Technologies were engaged in operating online Real Money Games (RMGs)  through the said apps and they had a user base of around 3 crore players primarily in the States of Telangana, Andhra Pradesh and Tamil Nadu.

title: Bhojshala row: Supreme Court to hear Muslim side's plea over location of alternative namaz site on July 29
content: The senior advocate alleged that “two Friday prayers were already missed, and the (allocated) site is 1.3 km away”; Solicitor-General Tushar Mehta opposed the submissions, saying, “It is 900 metres away”

title: Cyberabad’s Serilingampally to get Centre  for wildlife awareness, rescue and rehabilitation
content: Cyberabad Municipal Corporation (CMC) Standing Committee approves box drains, road projects in various parts, and development of PJR Stadium

title: Tamil Nadu CM Vijay proposes meeting with Karnataka counterpart D.K. Shivakumar over Cauvery
content: Even after accounting for the deficient rainfall during the current Southwest monsoon, Tamil Nadu is estimated to be entitled to receive at least three thousand million cubic feet (tmc ft.) more, according to a representative of the State government

title: Watch: Big bureaucratic reshuffle in Education Ministry amid NEET scrutiny
content: The Centre has announced a major bureaucratic reshuffle in the Education Ministry, with Naresh Pal Gangwar appointed as the new Higher Education Secretary.

title: Wayanad debris slip: Priyanka Gandhi to visit Kalladi, meet injured victims on July 25
content: Wayanad MP’s visit comes amid criticism from political opponents, including CPI(M) and BJP, who have questioned her absence in the immediate aftermath of the disaster on July 7

title: Mumbai street artist Tyler on his stencil becoming a protest symbol at Jantar Mantar
content: Mumbai-based street artist Tyler discusses their views on how their stencil of Prime Minister Narendra Modi flanked by industrialists became a protest symbol at the national protest against NEET examination paper leaks

title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: 'Pradhan's resignation non-negotiable', says CJP in meeting with Union Ministers
content: Union Health Minister J.P. Nadda and Minister of State for PMO Jitendra Singh represented the Government during the talks

title: Ex-sarpanch beaten to death in Uttar Pradesh's Ballia
content: Ram Vyas Verma, who was also a local Samajwadi Party leader, was found dead at a pump station with head injuries caused by a blunt object

title: SC proposes special courts for speedy trial in Manipur violence cases
content: Top court asks CBI and SIT to expedite pending investigations, proposes day-to-day trials, and directs that chargesheets be shared with victims’ families

title: 100 booked over Delhi student protest solidarity meet attended by rapper Vedan in Kerala’s Kochi
content: The accused have been booked on charges of obstructing public way, disobedience to lawful orders and unlawful assembly; Vignesh G. Pillai, reportedly the organiser of the event, is the prime accused

title: Youngsters are not afraid of CBI or ED: KTR tells Modi
content: “Promising ‘Make in India’, this government had delivered ‘Leak in India’,” BRS working president says in a sarcastic tone

title: Resignation of Pradhan non-negotiable: CJP to Union Ministers
content: Union Health Minister Nadda said the ministers heard their demands and conveyed that they will get back to the CJP. "We will again meet on Saturday," he said.

title: Gujarat rains displace 38,000 people; Army, NDRF launch rescue operations
content: As many as five trains towards Mumbai were cancelled owing to waterlogging in rail routes in south Gujarat

title: Utilise surplus lands of govt depts Indiramma houses in CURE; don’t develop colonies in isolated locations: Telangana CM
content: The estimated construction cost of each flat,of about 528 sq. ft., is around ₹11 lakh per unit, with the government providing a ₹5 lakh subsidy and beneficiaries contributing the remaining ₹6 lakh

title: PFI leader arrested in Kerala RSS activist Sreenivasan murder case remanded
content: Muhammadali K.P., PFI Palakkad district president, remanded after being produced at the residence of judge P.K. Mohandas, Special Judge, considering the cases booked by NIA in Kochi

title: T.N. govt. orders cancellation of ST certificates issued outside notified areas to Kanikkaran/Kanikkar community
content: The Revenue Authority of one district would not be competent to issue such a certificate in respect of persons belonging to another district, according to a G.O. issued in 2005

title: AMMK submits petition to T.N. Speaker seeking disqualification of Mannargudi MLA Kamaraj
content: On May 12, Mr. Dhinakaran expelled Mr. Kamaraj from the party for anti-party activities. The next day, the MLA voted in favour of the confidence motion moved by the Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay-led government.

title: Kerala BDS student death: HC slams police for failing to follow protocol in arrest of professor M.K. Ram
content: Court observes that guidelines issued by SC requiring to provide written grounds of arrest was not given to Dr. Ram, which led to his release by Special Court. This was affirmed by a report from Additional Director General of Police

title: Delhi HC declines interim injunction against OpenAI in ANI copyright suit
content: OpenAI’s act of storing ANI’s literary works for training its Large Language Models, prima facie, did not amount to copyright infringement, says judge

title: Kerala govt forms high-level panel to draft Sidharthan Anti-Ragging and Student Welfare Act
content: Kerala State Higher Education Council vice-chairperson Achuthsankar S. Nair to chair the committee constituted by Home Department

title: Supreme Court bars posting, uploading court proceedings on social media without prior permission
content: Senior advocate Vikas Singh, appearing for the petitioner, highlighted the misuse of videos of court proceedings on social media and other digital platforms

title: Kerala CPI(M) leader P.K Sreemathy to file criminal and civil defamation cases over ‘fake pension’ campaign
content: The former Kerala Health Minister says the misinformation had caused serious damage to her reputation built over five decades of public life

title: Mittaganahalli landfill, sitting on 287 crore litres of leachate, leaks into Srinivasapura Lake
content: The landfill is located at a higher elevation than the lake, and the leachate flows through a natural waterway before entering drain lines created for a nearby private layout, which then joins a natural drain, ultimately ending at the lake inlet

title: Yuva Morcha protest march to Kerala CM’s official residence over PSC exam ‘anomalies’ turns violent
content: Bharatiya Janata Yuva Morcha alleges double standards on the part of V.D. Satheesan in taking part in protest led by Rahul Gandhi outside PM Modi's residence in Delhi demanding action on NEET paper leaks

title: 'Beginning of accountability': Wangchuk says he extended fast by two days during negotiations
content: According to the post, Mr. Wangchuk ended his fast after 26 days upon getting assurance from the government and MPs of all political parties that the issue of accountability in the ‘failing’ examination system would be discussed in Parliament

title: As temporary tariffs expire, U.S. imposes 10% ‘forced labour’ tariffs on India
content: The new tariffs are a result of the findings of its investigations on whether the U.S.’ trade partners were doing enough to prevent the import of goods made using forced labour. A separate investigation on excess capacity is pending and could attract further tariffs

title: Brigade moves Madras High Court against revocation of environmental clearance for Pallikaranai project
content: Justice Mohammed Shaffiq directs the State Environment Impact Assessment Authority to explain how the clearance could be revoked unilaterally without notice to the realtor

title: Students march through central Hyderabad over NEET row, demand Union Education Minister’s resignation
content: Students participating in protest as part of Telangana-wide bandh call condemn police action against protesters during the recent Parliament march in Delhi

title: Vijay’s CMO to be shifted to Namakkal Kavignar Maaligai due to space constraints
content: An official said that even after the CMO is relocated, Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay may occasionally use the existing Chief Minister’s chamber in the main block.

title: How Kerala keeps its Karkkadakam rituals alive
content: Even as times change and climate vagaries reshape Kerala, certain rituals that mark Karkkadakam continue to hold sway

title: Karnataka Deputy Chief Minister G. Parameshwara writes to Modi seeking replacement of NEET with State CETs
content: The Deputy Chief Minister appeals to the Prime Minister to provide a compensation of at least ₹1 crore to the families of students who ended their lives owing to the NEET paper leak and the “systemic failures” that followed.

title: What is Tamil Nadu’s new presence-less property registration service? | Explained
content: The Tamil Nadu government is set to make property registration easier with its new ‘Presence-less Registration’ system, launching on August 17, 2026. The move, in the first phase, will enable online property registration by flat and plot promoters, bankers and financial institutions eliminating visits to Sub-Registrar offices.

title: Vivek Shanbhag on ‘Hyphen’, and bridging the gap in India’s translation landscape
content: Novelist Vivek Shanbhag speaks about the launch of Hyphen, a biannual journal focused on translations from Indian languages into English, and why the journal is an efficient way to introduce Indian writing to readers around the world

title: Parliament Monsoon Session Day 5 highlights: Both Houses adjourned for the day amid Oppn. protests; Bill to give Vande Mataram legal protection introduced in RS
content: Kiren Rijiju urged Rahul Gandhi and Opposition MPs to allow for Lok Sabha to function; reiterated that the Govt. is willing to take up NEET discussion

title: Media reports 'reckless', no plea related to CJP-led protests filed: CJI
content: On July 22, a lawyer sought urgent hearing on the police action against students during the Cockroach Janta Party-led protest march in New Delhi on July 20.

title: Man allegedly kills wife over Talliki Vandanam money in Annamayya district
content: Padmavathi, 28, of Kothapalli village was allegedly killed by her husband after she refused to hand over money credited under the Talliki Vandanam scheme, her mother told the police; a case has been registered

title: Education loan can be denied if parent as co-borrower has poor credit score, says Kerala HC
content: However, the application could be considered if there is an eligible co-borrower with sufficient credit score, says High Court

title: NHAI revises toll charges on Bengaluru-Nelamangala and Bengaluru-Kolar highway stretches
content: According to separate public notices issued by the Project Implementation Unit (PIU), Bengaluru, the revised toll rates will be applicable at the Bengaluru-Nelamangala toll plazas on NH-48 and the Hedaginabele, Agrahara, Krishnarajapura and Sundarapalya toll plazas on the Bengaluru-Bethamangala section of NH-75

title: Protests held across Uttar Pradesh over NEET paper leak case and other examination irregularities
content: In Ghazipur, Varanasi, Ballia, Azamgarh, protests held by a group of students, who demanded the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan and revamping the examination process

title: Officials tell GMR to finish pending works at Bhogapuram airport before Modi’s visit
content: Special Chief Secretary M.T. Krishna Babu inspected the site with Collector S. Ramsundar Reddy and Joint Collector S. Sedhu Madhavan; around 2,000 buses will be arranged and 6,000 private vehicles are expected on the day

title: Not holding NEET debate will send wrong message to country: Rijiju in Lok Sabha
content: The Minister also noted that activist Sonam Wangchuk has broken his fast and the House should begin a debate on the subject

title: Bengaluru STRR nears completion as four of six packages finished, remaining stretches due this year
content: The update was provided by Union Minister for Road Transport and Highways Nitin Gadkari in a written reply to an unstarred question raised by member Ajay Maken in the Rajya Sabha on Wednesday (July 22).

title: Farmers oppose acquisition of land for HAL expansion at Gubbi in Tumakuru district
content: Farmers are worried that HAL’s long-term plans to expand the facility by adding infrastructure such as a hospital and other amenities for its employees could lead to further acquisition of agricultural land

title: CJP to launch website to identify police personnel who used brute force on protestors during Parliament March
content: Condemning the allegations of ‘foreign funding’ behind the protest Sourav Das said, “If narrative is being pushed that we are foreign-funded, why is the government engaging with Sonam Wangchuk? Why is the government engaging with us?”

title: Major fire breaks out at plastic recycling factory in Kerala’s Kasaragod; none injured
content: GAIL natural gas pipeline runs beneath factory premises at a depth of about 2 m. After fire was extinguished, officials inspected the area above the underground pipeline and confirmed that it had not been affected

title: IndiGo, Air India oppose Adani’s airline entry
content: It is a recipe for conflict of interest as well as squeezing other players; such a consolidation across the value chain not only kills competition but also results in fewer jobs, said an Air India official

title: Don’t cultivate paddy this kharif, Andhra Pradesh tells Krishna delta farmers amid water crunch
content: Engineer-in-Chief K. Narasimha Murthy said storage in Srisailam and Nagarjuna Sagar had fallen to critically low levels, and that Pattiseema pumping may last only 60 to 70 days this season against 100 to 120 days earlier; farmers have been advised to switch to dry crops

title: Two 4th century Ganesha idols found in a field at Kaza near Amaravati
content: Archaeologist E. Sivanagireddy said the red sandstone idols, seated in lalitasana and holding a modaka and a broken tusk, resemble a Ganesha image of the same period from Chejarla, the Ananda Gotrika capital

title: How should cities reclaim footpaths? | Explained
content: Why was the Street Vendors Act enacted? What does the Act require before vendors can be removed? What is the role of the Town Vending Committee? Can street vendors be evicted before a survey is completed? Why has Bengaluru’s footpath drive come under scrutiny?

title: Wanted ‘Waris Punjab De’ member, U.S. national nabbed while entering India from Nepal
content: The accused were identified as Vikramjit Singh, who is wanted in connection with a 2023 case registered in Punjab, and U.S. national Manveer Singh, a native of Punjab

title: Kerala govt issues order suspending ADGP M.R. Ajith Kumar, cites SIT findings of ‘interference' in Youth Congress workers assault probe
content: Govt holds that allegations against senior IPS officer are supported by statements of multiple investigating officers and that his continuance in service can affect fairness of inquiry

title: Rhiya Ahir: How an Army man’s daughter became Mumbai’s face of resistance
content: The powerful image of a single girl stopping a police van with one hand was splashed across social media, catapulting her into an overnight sensation. ‘I was ready to be a martyr that day,’ says Rhiya Ahir

title: Centre moves to block Bitchat over terror, surveillance concerns amid CJP protest
content: India's cybercrime center has asked GitHub to remove the Bitchat application. This app allows anonymous, offline messaging which raises security concerns for the government. Officials believe it could be misused by terrorists and criminal groups for coordination. The notice cites potential violations of the Information Technology Act. This action follows the use of similar apps during recent Delhi protests.

title: TOI Exclusive: Olympian Tulika Maan provisionally suspended by NADA, set to miss Glasgow CWG
content: India's Commonwealth Games campaign has suffered a setback after Olympian and Birmingham 2022 silver medallist Tulika Maan was provisionally suspended by NADA for alleged whereabouts failures. The suspension rules her out of Glasgow 2026, with the heavyweight judoka facing anti-doping proceedings that could result in a ban of up to two years if upheld.

title: US introduced new bill that may get Mercedes-Benz 'banned' from selling cars in America
content: A new US Senate bill may ban Chinese automakers and their components from American roads. This legislation could affect Mercedes-Benz due to its significant Chinese investment. The bill aims to protect American manufacturing and competitiveness from foreign threats. Mercedes-Benz will have until 2030 to comply with the new ownership rules. Other companies like Polestar also face potential sales restrictions under similar regulations.

title: US new visa restriction targets individuals involved in cybercrime
content: The United States has introduced a new global visa restriction policy. This measure targets individuals involved in cybercrime and cyber-enabled offenses. Online investment scams and sextortion perpetrators face these new restrictions. Immediate family members of those responsible may also be subjected to visa limitations. The administration is deploying various tools to dismantle criminal scam networks.

title: Ireland recovers remains of 99 infants from former Tuam mother and baby home
content: Ireland has recovered ninety-nine infant remains from a former mother and baby home.  Forensic excavations are ongoing at the Tuam site, revealing hidden burial chambers.  DNA analysis aims to identify the children and restore their identities to families.  This effort acknowledges a dark chapter in the country's institutional history.  The state is now confronting its past and treating the deceased with dignity.

title: Meet Michael Stücke, German farmer rescuing gay sheep from slaughter
content: A German farmer rescues gay sheep, which are often discarded by the agricultural industry.  His Rainbow Wool project transforms these animals into symbols for animal and human rights.  Profits from wool sales and collaborations support the LSVD+ foundation, aiding refugees.  This initiative highlights the naturalness of homosexuality and challenges societal prejudices.  The project continues its vital work, advocating for acceptance and safety globally.

title: Briine Pacific Tide sparkling water recalled in Alberta over bulging cans
content: The Government of Canada has recalled Briine Pacific Tide Sparkling Spring Waterin Alberta after some355 ml canswere found to be bulging. The recall has been issued due tonon-harmful microbial contamination (quality or spoilage). Consumers and retailers have been advised not to use, sell, serve or distribute the affected product and to check thetwo affected best-before codes: 01AL2028 and 02AL2028.

title: 10% US tariff on India: What it means for the country's exports
content: United States imposed a ten percent tariff on Indian goods, offering some relief. Economists believe the immediate impact on India's exports will be limited. Reducing dependence on single markets and broadening global reach is crucial. Certainty after months of shifting proposals is a positive development. Sustained export growth depends on competitiveness and diversification efforts.

title: Delhi HC refuses to hear plea seeking NIA, CBI probe into July 20 protest march
content: The Delhi High Court declined a PIL seeking a probe into a recent protest march.  Authorities already know about the incident and can act if needed.  The Cockroach Janta Party continues talks with the government over NEET examination demands.  Their key demand for the Union Education Minister's resignation remains non-negotiable.  Further discussions are scheduled as the NEET paper leak fallout continues.

title: 'Dhamaal 4' BO Day 15: Targets Rs 140 crore India collection
content: Ajay Devgn-led comedy, 'Dhamaal 4' continues its theatrical momentum after a solid second week. After crossing Rs. 136.65 crore India net by Day 14, the film has begun Day 15 of its box office run. Here's a detailed breakdown of its day-wise earnings, worldwide box office performance, next milestones, and whether it can sustain momentum despite competition from Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey'.

title: An offer they can't refuse: Don's protection racket in the Middle East & Europe
content: Donald Trump's approach to international security resembles a protection racket, insisting that allies contribute more for American military support and armament. This transactional style echoes his history in real estate. Countries in the Gulf and Nato are responding to pressure for increased defense expenditures, turning their anxiety into financial gain for the United States.

title: Consumer court pulls up internet provider over repeated outages, awards Rs 10K to customer
content: A Himachal Pradesh consumer commission ordered JioFiber to pay compensation.  The company faced charges for deficiency in service and unfair trade practice.  Repeated internet outages went unresolved despite multiple customer complaints.  JioFiber failed to provide adequate technical evidence to prove issue resolution.  The commission directed the company to restore the connection and pay damages.

title: 'Reckless': CJI rejects reports of refusing hearing on police action against students
content: Chief Justice of India Surya Kant denied reports of refusing a petition. He clarified that no formal petition challenging police action was filed. A lawyer had mentioned a letter representation before the court. The clarification came two days after a lawyer mentioned the issue before a bench headed by the CJI and sought an urgent hearing over the alleged use of force against students during the July 20 protest.

title: Spain invokes first wildfire emergency as fires rage across Europe; over 10,000 evacuated
content: Spain declared a national emergency due to widespread wildfires. France requested European Union assistance for a major blaze near Bordeaux. Tens of thousands were evacuated from affected tourist and residential areas. This crisis highlights the intensifying impact of climate change on southern Europe. The region is experiencing record-breaking heat and drought conditions.

title: 'I am not taking it for granted': Sooryavanshi after breaking Sachin's record
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi achieved a record-breaking international half-century against Zimbabwe. This significant innings provided much-needed confidence for the young player. He became the youngest to score a fifty in men's international cricket. Sooryavanshi dedicated his milestone to his supportive family and coaches. India secured a seven-wicket victory in the opening T20I match.

title: Faster drones, cruise missile boost: What DRDO’s new turbojet engine can power
content: India's DRDO GTRE has developed a new 350kg thrust expendable turbojet engine.  This engine is crucial for modern warfare, powering cruise missiles and target drones.  The development supports India's growing capabilities in indigenous defense technologies.  This advancement reduces reliance on foreign imports for critical defense components.  It also enhances the nation's strategic autonomy in defense manufacturing.

title: 'Will monitor throughout the year': SC assures 'institutionalisation' of NEET reforms
content: ​​A bench of Justices PS Narasimha and Alok Aradhe said the affidavit must explain the steps taken to institutionalise the reforms instead of relying on ad hoc measures. Justice Narasimha noted that the deployment of the Indian Air Force (for safe transportation of questions) after the paper leak was only an ad hoc measure and could not be a permanent solution.

title: What's the Pacific Decadal Oscillation linked to Gujarat's heavy rains?
content: The Pacific Decadal Oscillation (PDO) has gained attention after scientists linked it to conditions that may have influenced Gujarat's recent heavy rainfall. This explainer outlines what the long-term Pacific climate pattern is, how it differs from El Niño, its phases, and why understanding the PDO is important for interpreting monsoon behaviour and extreme weather events.

title: 'Tied up her minor boyfriend': NEET aspirant from Satna gang-raped in UP
content: A 20-year-old NEET aspirant was allegedly gang-raped in Uttar Pradesh's Chitrakoot. Three men, posing as forest officials, overpowered and tied up her minor boyfriend. The incident occurred on Wednesday in the forested Devangana Valley area. The boyfriend freed himself and alerted police after finding mobile network coverage. Police have detained one suspect and are searching for two others.

title: NYC Upper West Side stabbing: Zohran Mamdani reveals disturbing details of 'hateful' attack
content: New York City police are investigating two recent stabbings on Manhattan's Upper West Side.  Witnesses reported the suspect shouted "Allahu Akbar" during both assaults on victims.  Authorities have arrested 51-year-old Raul Morales in connection with these separate incidents.  Investigators are examining mental health factors and whether these attacks qualify as hate crimes.

title: 'Jana Nayagan' collection: Vijay's farewell film reaches another milestone
content: Jana Nayagan's second day collections reached Rs 49 crore India gross. The film is performing very well with packed shows nationwide. Tamil Nadu remains the biggest market for Vijay's farewell film. Weekend collections are expected to boost the movie's earnings further. Audiences continue to throng theaters for the popular actor's movie.

title: ‘I thought he was only trying to scare me’: Youth in the line of pellet gun fire at CJP protest
content: A 25-year-old CJP protester, Irshad Shaikh, who was allegedly hit by pellet gun fire during the ‘Sansad Chalo’ march, recalled the moments after he was injured near Jantar Mantar. Shaikh alleged an RAF staffer fired at him after clashes broke out. He suffered pellet injuries to his face, neck and upper body and underwent surgery.

title: Project Kusha explained: Why India’s 'own S-400' is not designed to stop ballistic missiles
content: India’s maiden test of the Kusha Surface-to-Air Missile marks a decisive step in building a layered air defence shield under Mission Sudarshan Chakra. Designed in three variants—M1 (150 km), M2 (250 km) and M3 (350 km)—the system aims to rival the Russian S-400 but with indigenous scalability. The test underscores DRDO’s ability to intercept threats in both endo- and exo-atmospheric domains. Yet, as Gulf conflicts show, missile defence requires redundancy and integration. India must now invest in sensors, lasers, and command networks to ensure resilience against saturation attacks.

title: Historic first at Commonwealth Games as Braille medals debut in Glasgow
content: Glasgow 2026 will introduce historic medals with braille and tactile elements for visually impaired athletes.  This innovation ensures a more inclusive experience for all competitors during the games.  The medals feature a unique Reuleaux triangle shape inspired by Glasgow's coat of arms.  Engraved patterns and textured finishes reflect local landmarks and Scottish heritage.  This marks a significant step towards greater accessibility in the Commonwealth Games.

title: Ordered iPhone, got beard oil: Mumbai man wins Rs 2.14 lakh from Flipkart, seller
content: A consumer commission ordered Flipkart and a seller to refund Rs 2.14 lakh.  The Powai resident received beard growth oil instead of an iPhone 14 Pro.  This compensation covers the phone's price and mental agony.  Flipkart faced ex parte proceedings for failing to resolve the issue.  Apple India was dismissed from the case as the dispute was about delivery.

title: Why Gujarat HC ordered Muslim family to shift buried body to community graveyard
content: Gujarat high court ruled a Muslim family cannot bury relatives on disputed land. A designated community graveyard with sufficient space was available in Bhavnagar. The court dismissed the family's plea and upheld a panchayat notice to shift the body. Inspection reports confirmed ample space remained in the Muslim graveyard. The family agreed to relocate the body to the designated burial site.

title: Cop rapes policeman's daughter, class 10 student as colleague keeps watch; both arrested
content: Two married police constables face charges for abducting and raping a teenager.  They lured the Class 10 student to a vacant room under false pretenses.  The constables then fled after the alleged assault occurred on Wednesday evening.  The survivor reported the incident to her parents immediately after returning home.  Police have booked and arrested the accused officers for their alleged actions.

title: After DU, JNU asks its students, staff to avoid Jantar Mantar protest site
content: "​​Advisory All stakeholders of JNU’s epistemic community are advised to act responsibly and prioritize their personal safety. In accordance with the Hon’ble Supreme Court of India’s directions on public demonstrations, you are requested to refrain from participating in or visiting gatherings at or around Jantar Mantar, New Delhi," the University posted on X. ​Earlier, the University of Delhi (DU) has issued an official "safety advisory".

title: Ashes in India? Cricket Australia leaves door open for landmark 150th Test
content: Cricket Australia is open to staging the 150th anniversary Ashes Test in India. This reflects the growing importance of the Indian cricket market globally. The Big Bash League will also stage a match in Chennai this December. Hosting a marquee contest in India offers significant commercial opportunities. Scheduling challenges exist due to India's packed cricket calendar.

title: 40 minutes, 1 van, a city watching: Mumbai woman's photo captures resolve behind CJP protests
content: A young woman blocked a police van carrying detained protesters in Mumbai. Rhiya Ahir stood before the vehicle for forty minutes demanding their release. She questioned the authorities' handling of demonstrations and police actions. Ahir stated the system has failed citizens in many ways. Younger generations are now raising their voices against injustice.

title: Crackdown on paper leak: Govt planning to tweak anti-cheating law by increasing jail term
content: In the expected amendment, the government is likely to increase the jail for the offenders from the current 10-year ceiling in the proposed Bill, which the Cabinet will take up this afternoon, sources told TOI. The Public Examinations (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act, 2024 was enacted to curb paper leaks, cheating, and organised malpractices in public examinations like UPSC, SSC, and NTA tests provides for penalties, including up to 10 years in prison and a Rs 1 crore fine.

title: Father's 'Govardhan Parikrama' in Mathura pays off as Mayank makes fiery return
content: Two years after injuries threatened to derail his career, Mayank Yadav announced his return with a fiery 2 for 18 against Zimbabwe. While his father prayed for his fitness during the Govardhan Parikrama in Mathura, the Delhi pacer touched 149 kph and reminded everyone why the Indian cricket board never stopped believing in him.

title: Court Junks Plea For Anti-Terror Agency Probe In July 20 Parliament March
content: The bench also said it was for the Centre to refer any case to the National Investigation Agency (NIA) for investigation, and not the court.

title: "Can't Let This Go On Like This": Supreme Court To Centre On NEET Paper Leak Row
content: A bench of Justices PS Narasimha and Alok Aradhe, while hearing the NEET paper leaks issue, told the Centre that they would be monitoring the issue "very closely".

title: 2 Doctors Drown While Trying To Retrieve Phone From Jharkand Lake
content: One of the doctors entered the reservoir to retrieve the phone but began drowning after venturing into deep water, MGM Hospital's Superintendent Dr Balram Jha said.

title: Instagram To Ban Creepy POV Videos Shot With Meta Glasses
content: Instagram head said videos filmed with smart glasses that harass or exploit people will be taken down, marking one of the platform's strongest moves yet against invasive POV (point-of-view) content.

title: Grand Egyptian Museum's Architects To Build Startup Museum In Bengaluru
content: The Karnataka Technology Innovation Museum Foundation has been formed as the legal entity to execute the project.

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex Settles 330 Points Lower As Brent Crude Tops $100 Amid US-Iran War
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty Highlights: Oil prices back at $100: Brent crude hits 2-month high as Red Sea attacks stoke supply fears.

title: CJP Protest LIVE Updates: Members Of Cockroach Janta Party, Centre Conclude 2nd Round Of Talks
content: CJP Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: Activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his 26-day fast at Gurugram's Medanta Hospital last night after the government gave him assurances.

title: Weather LIVE Updates: Delhi Records Below-Normal Day Temperature, Light Rain Likely Next Week
content: Weather Today, Delhi-NCR Rain, Monsoon Live Updates: Light drizzle expected in Delhi NCR with cloudy skies and max temperature 34.5C today

title: Alcohol, Cannabis, Xanax: Saudi Prince Died At London Hotel After Taking Cocktail Of Drugs
content: A cleaner found the Saudi royal lying on the bathroom floor of his locked fifth-floor room, fully clothed. Paramedics were rushed but were unable to resuscitate him.

title: Adani Energy Solutions Wins Rs 8,500 Crore Andhra Power Transmission Project
content: The project will also support the growing load requirement expected from the Pendurthi-Vizag region, where data centre and digital infrastructure investments are taking shape.

title: Gujarat Highway Shut As Rain Triggers Flooding, Army Joins Rescue Ops
content: The rescue efforts have been launched near Meldi Mata temple and the Gallops Industrial Part area close to Sariya Patiya.

title: "Dharmendra Pradhan Must Quit": Abhijeet Dipke As CJP, Centre Hold Talks
content: When pointed out what if Sonam Wangchuk, who ended his 26-day hunger strike late on Thursday night following government assurances on his key demands, asks him to call off the protest, Abhijeet Dipke answered in the negative.

title: Parliament Monsoon Session 2026 Highlights: Lok Sabha Adjourned Till Monday, Centre Says Not Holding NEET Debate Will Send Wrong Message
content: Parliament Monsoon Session 2026 Highlights: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has also assured of a stringent law on paper leaks and holding trial of the accused in fast-track courts, he said, while also noting that activist Sonam Wangchuk has ended his hunger strike.

title: 8th Pay Commission Restarts Talks, Fresh August Meeting Dates Announced
content: The 8th Pay Commission, constituted in November 2025, has been tasked with recommending revisions to salaries, pensions and allowances.

title: Philippines Accuses China Of Water Cannon Use As Clashes Continue For 2nd Day
content: "The PCG strongly condemns the dangerous and unprofessional actions of the China Coast Guard," saysspokesperson Jay Tarriela.

title: Pak's Asim Munir Used A Private Oil Deal In Iran To Shield Saudi: Report
content: Asim Munir mediated the Saudi-Iran deal by dangling the continued operation of a joint oil transport venture with the IRGC Chief, which has been quietly transporting oil from Iran to Pakistan since late last year.

title: "Shards Stuck To Skin": Hospital Report Shows Pellet Guns Used At CJP Protest
content: The patient's case sheet at Safdarjung Hospital details his injuries: "darkened skin around wounds, pellet shards stuck to skin and tenderness on impact sites".

title: Weeks After RBI Raised Concerns, Parliament Panel Proposes Crypto Watchdog
content: Recommendation comes at a time when cryptocurrencies are taxed in India and exchanges share transaction data with anti-money laundering authorities.

title: "Saw Cop Pointing His Pellet Gun At Me": Man Injured In CJP Protest To NDTV
content: The man was injured during a protest march on July 20, called by the Cockroach Janta Party, or CJP, demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the NEET paper leak.

title: Paper Leak "Students' Issue, Shouldn't Be Politicised": Assam Minister
content: The protesters said the demonstration was aimed at seeking justice for students affected by the controversy.
